void_entry.text: The world ended at the threshold.
Beyond it — not darkness. Absence. A space where reality had been scraped away, leaving only the raw substrate of nothing.
Fragments of ground floated in the void. Stone from somewhere that no longer existed. Paths that led between things that weren't there.

void_entry.speaker: Elia
void_entry.text: The air... it's not thin. There just isn't any.

void_entry.speaker: Elia
void_entry.text: That's what worries me. You shouldn't be able to.
The Void Hole designated BL-07. This is what happens when the world forgets too much in one place.

void_core.text: At the center, the tear widened. Not a hole — a mouth. Open and patient.
Something pulsed inside it. Not light. Not sound. A rhythm that his bones recognized even if his mind didn't.
He could feel it now. The Void Hole wasn't destroying. It was forgetting. The world was forgetting itself here, and the hole was what was left.
And somewhere in his chest, the Grade 1 memory — 'The Name Arrel' — hummed in resonance.

void_core.speaker: Arrel
void_core.text: I know how to close it.

void_core.speaker: Arrel
void_core.text: The same way I do everything. Burn something that matters.

seal_decision.text: The core of BL-07 pulsed. Waiting.

seal_decision.speaker: Elia
seal_decision.text: You're talking about burning your name. Your NAME, Arrel. The last thing that makes you... you.

seal_decision.speaker: Arrel
seal_decision.text: If The Seam falls, everyone there loses everything. Not just a name.

seal_decision.speaker: Elia
seal_decision.text: And if you burn it? What's left?

seal_decision.speaker: Arrel
seal_decision.text: I don't know. That's the point.
The Void Hole hummed. Patient. Hungry. Indifferent to the choice being made at its edge.

choices.text: Keep the Name — Find another way

seal_complete.text: He reached inside. Past the memories of rain and campfires and a hand reaching out. Past the sword and the market and the song.
Down to the bottom. To the name.
The fire was not blue this time. It was white. Pure. The color of everything and nothing at once.
The Void Hole screamed — a sound that existed only in the space between hearing and remembering — and collapsed inward.
The ground beneath them solidified. Color bled back into the stone. The crack sealed like a wound closing.
The man who had been Arrel opened his eyes. He looked at the woman standing over him.
But somewhere — deeper than memory, deeper than names — something in him knew she was important.

seal_complete.speaker: Elia
seal_complete.text: Do you... do you know who you are?
He thought about it for a long time.
No. But I know I'm supposed to be here.
BL-07 was sealed. The Seam would survive. The cost was everything that made the price meaningful.

seal_refused.text: He pulled his hand back from the edge.

seal_refused.speaker: Arrel
seal_refused.text: There has to be another way. There always is. People just stop looking because burning is easier.
The Void Hole pulsed. Unimpressed. Unchanged.
They walked back the way they came. The fragments of ground felt less stable than before. Or maybe that was just the weight of a decision unmade.

seal_refused.speaker: Arrel
seal_refused.text: Sable doesn't have to lose her name.
Behind them, BL-07 continued to grow. Slowly. Patiently.
They had time. Not much. But some. And sometimes 'some' is enough to find what 'always' couldn't.

void_descent.text: The path — if it could be called that — descended in spirals that didn't obey geometry.
Each step took them deeper. Not into the ground. Into the absence of ground. The fragments they walked on were memories of stone, held together by the Void's habit of destruction.
On a floating shard to their left — a door. Standing upright, attached to nothing. An ordinary wooden door with a brass handle, impossibly preserved.

void_descent.speaker: Elia
void_descent.text: Was. Before the Void ate the house around it. The door is all that's left.
As they passed, the door swung open. Beyond it — nothing. Just more nothing. But from nothing came a sound: a child calling for dinner. A mother answering.
Then silence. The door closed itself, gently, as if the memory had finished its performance and was taking a bow.

void_echoes.text: Deeper in, the Void began to speak.
Not words. Feelings. Borrowed grief that pressed against his ribs like an unwelcome hand.
He tasted copper. He smelled woodsmoke. He heard his own name spoken in a voice he didn't recognize — and felt a certainty that the voice was important.

void_echoes.speaker: Elia
void_echoes.text: It shows everyone things. That's how it feeds. You feel the loss, and the feeling makes the loss bigger, and the bigger loss feeds the Void.
She was quiet for three steps.

void_echoes.speaker: Elia
void_echoes.text: You. Before the burns. The person you were when we first met.

void_echoes.speaker: Elia
void_echoes.text: You laughed more. Did you know that? You used to laugh at terrible things. It drove me insane.

void_echoes.speaker: Elia
void_echoes.text: You smile. It's not the same thing.
The Void hummed around them, drinking the sadness like water.

void_memory_fragments.text: Scattered across a floating platform — dozens of crystallized memories, glowing faintly in colors that shouldn't exist.
Each one was someone's life. A first step. A last breath. A morning so ordinary that no one thought to protect it from being forgotten.

void_memory_fragments.speaker: Elia
void_memory_fragments.text: These are the memories the Void has consumed. It doesn't destroy them — it KEEPS them. Like a collection.

void_memory_fragments.speaker: Elia
void_memory_fragments.text: They don't belong to anyone anymore. The people who lost them have forgotten they ever existed. A memory without an owner is just... light.
He picked one up. Warm. It showed him a sunset over a city he'd never seen, felt through eyes that were not his own. Beautiful. Meaningless. Orphaned.
He set it down. Carefully. Even orphaned beauty deserved to be handled gently.

void_before_core.text: The pull was stronger here. Like standing at the edge of a waterfall, except the water was made of forgetting.

void_before_core.speaker: Elia
void_before_core.text: Arrel. Before we go further. There's something I need to say.

void_before_core.speaker: Elia
void_before_core.text: Whatever happens in there — whatever you decide — I need you to know that the person you are RIGHT NOW is enough. You don't have to burn anything else to be worth saving.

void_before_core.speaker: Arrel
void_before_core.text: It's not about being worth saving. It's about the Seam. The children. Sable's people.

void_before_core.speaker: Elia
void_before_core.text: I know. But the world doesn't get to have you AND those children. It shouldn't have to choose. Something is WRONG with a world that makes people pay for survival with themselves.
Her voice echoed in the absence. The Void drank the sound, but the meaning survived.

void_before_core.speaker: Arrel
void_before_core.text: Are you trying to talk me out of it?

void_before_core.speaker: Elia
void_before_core.text: I'm trying to make sure that whatever you choose, you choose it as ARREL. Not as a weapon. Not as a tool. As the person who once laughed at terrible things and noticed flowers growing in impossible places.
The core of BL-07 waited ahead. Patient as gravity. Hungry as absence.
He looked at Elia. She looked back. Two people standing at the edge of everything, holding onto the only thing the Void couldn't take — the choice to be here, together, right now.

elia_void_talk.speaker: Elia
elia_void_talk.text: I keep trying to remember what this place reminds me of. But there's nothing TO remember. It's... new.

elia_void_talk.speaker: Elia
elia_void_talk.text: I've never been in a place that has no past. Everything has a past. Even ruins. Even ash.

elia_void_talk.speaker: Elia
elia_void_talk.text: But this... this is what happens when even the past forgets it was here.
She shivered. Not from cold. There was no temperature here. Only absence.
